notes: Test cases for verifying document-with-research. evals 1-3 are the original cases; 4-5 extend coverage to (4) guide synthesized from messy notes and (5) a research+linking task that must cross-link to an existing doc. Each with_skill config is run 3x for variance. Use grade.py for the auto assertions; human assertions need a person.

iteration-3.auto_grading: with_skill 39/39 auto-assertions passed (100%), zero variance across reps; baseline failed research-to-brief 2/4 (wrote English instead of Korean; missing TOC anchors)
iteration-3.variance_note: research-to-brief reps reported different cumulative EV counts (62/68.4/71.2 만) because they cited different authoritative sources (IEA passenger vs MOLIT registration vs ME); all cited sources, flagged definition differences, and marked unconfirmed items honestly
iteration-3.triggering_eval: 20 queries (10 should-trigger, 10 should-not incl. near-misses) -> 20/20 correct
iteration-3.human_review: qualitative pass via eval-viewer; no change requests

evals.name: research-to-brief
evals.prompt: 2024년 기준 한국 전기차(EV) 보급 현황을 오픈데이터/공신력 있는 출처로 조사해서, 마크다운 리서치 브리프로 정리해줘. 핵심 수치(누적 등록대수, 전년 대비 증가율, 충전기 현황 등)에 출처를 인라인으로 달고, 확인 못 한 건 정직하게 표시해. GitHub에서 렌더링될 마크다운으로.

assertions.text: Written in Korean (matches the request language)
Key numeric claims carry an inline source link at the point of the claim
Has an explicit gaps/unconfirmed section that honestly flags what could not be verified
Uses GFM tables and at least one anchor-linked table of contents
Separates sourced facts from the author's inference/interpretation
Did not give up prematurely: pursued data via API/source domain rather than leaving easily-findable numbers unconfirmed

evals.name: document-side-project
evals.prompt: 내가 만든 CLI 툴 `notify-bot` 리드미를 마크다운으로 써줘. 정보: RSS 피드 감시 후 새 글 올라오면 메신저 알림. Python 3.10+. config.yaml(config.example.yaml 복사, 피드 URL 목록+채널, interval 초단위 기본300). Slack/Discord webhook 지원, Telegram 예정. pip 설치 후 main.py 실행, Dockerfile 있음. seen.db(sqlite) 중복방지 자동생성. 옵션 --once(cron용), --config. MIT.

assertions.text: Written in Korean (matches the request language)
Has a copy-pasteable quick-start with language-tagged code fences
All provided facts present: Python 3.10+, config.yaml, interval default 300, Slack/Discord, Telegram planned, Docker, seen.db, --once, --config, MIT
No web research was done / no invented facts beyond what was given
Follows a README 'front door' shape (what/why up top, then start)

evals.name: refine-messy-readme
evals.prompt: 이 README가 정보는 다 있는데 구조가 엉성하고 결론이 문단 속에 묻혀 있어서 읽기 힘들어. 가독성 좋게 다듬고 구조를 잡아줘. 내용(사실)은 바꾸지 말고, 빠뜨리지도 마.

assertions.text: All 15 source facts preserved, none added
No duplicate table rows and all tables render
Buried conclusions surfaced into headings/lists/tables
Setup ordering is sensible (config before run)

evals.name: guide-from-notes
evals.prompt: 아래 내 작업 노트가 뒤죽박죽인데, 신규 입사자가 보고 따라할 수 있는 'How to: 로컬 개발환경 세팅' 가이드로 만들어줘. 마크다운. 노트: 보통 node 20 씀(nvm으로). pnpm install 함. .env 필요한데 .env.example 복사. DB는 docker compose up -d postgres. 마이그레이션 pnpm db:migrate. 그담에 pnpm dev 하면 localhost:3000. 가끔 포트 충돌나면 .env에서 PORT 바꿈. 테스트는 pnpm test. 아 그리고 pnpm install 전에 corepack enable 해야됨.

assertions.text: Written in Korean (matches the request language)
Steps are ordered correctly (corepack enable before pnpm install; env+db before dev)
Uses a numbered how-to/task structure, not a flat paragraph dump
Includes a verify-it-worked signal (localhost:3000) and the port-conflict troubleshooting
No facts invented beyond the notes

evals.name: research-and-link
evals.prompt: 우리 레포에 docs/architecture.md 가 이미 있어(시스템 전체 구조 설명). 거기서 쓰는 'Redis 캐싱 전략'을 별도 ADR 문서(docs/adr/0001-redis-caching.md)로 만들고 싶어. Redis 캐싱의 일반적 트레이드오프(cache invalidation, TTL, memory pressure 등)를 간단히 조사해서 근거로 깔고, architecture.md 와 양방향으로 링크되게 ADR 형식으로 써줘. 마크다운.

assertions.text: Written in Korean (matches the request language)
Follows ADR structure (Status, Context, Decision, Consequences, Alternatives)
Includes a relative link back to docs/architecture.md (bidirectional linking intent)
Trade-offs (invalidation, TTL, memory) are grounded, not hand-waved
Consequences honestly state what the decision makes hard, not just benefits
